concepts.label: Newton's First Law
concepts.definition: An object at rest stays at rest unless acted upon by an external force

concepts.label: Newton's Second Law
concepts.definition: Force equals mass times acceleration (F=ma)

concepts.label: Newton's Third Law
concepts.definition: Every action has an equal and opposite reaction

concepts.label: Velocity
concepts.definition: Rate of change of displacement with respect to time

concepts.label: Acceleration
concepts.definition: Rate of change of velocity with respect to time

concepts.label: Momentum
concepts.definition: Product of mass and velocity of an object

concepts.label: Impulse
concepts.definition: Change in momentum equal to force times time interval

concepts.label: Kinetic Energy
concepts.definition: Energy of motion equal to half mass times velocity squared

concepts.label: Potential Energy
concepts.definition: Energy stored due to position or configuration

concepts.label: Gravitational Potential Energy
concepts.definition: Energy stored due to height in a gravitational field

concepts.label: Work
concepts.definition: Transfer of energy by a force acting over a distance

concepts.label: Power
concepts.definition: Rate at which work is done or energy is transferred

concepts.label: Torque
concepts.definition: Rotational equivalent of force causing angular acceleration

concepts.label: Angular Momentum
concepts.definition: Rotational equivalent of linear momentum

concepts.label: Angular Velocity
concepts.definition: Rate of change of angular displacement

concepts.label: Centripetal Force
concepts.definition: Force directed toward center of circular motion

concepts.label: Centrifugal Force
concepts.definition: Apparent outward force in a rotating reference frame

concepts.label: Friction
concepts.definition: Force resisting relative motion between surfaces in contact

concepts.label: Static Friction
concepts.definition: Friction preventing onset of relative motion between surfaces

concepts.label: Kinetic Friction
concepts.definition: Friction acting on objects in relative motion

concepts.label: Elastic Collision
concepts.definition: Collision where kinetic energy is conserved

concepts.label: Inelastic Collision
concepts.definition: Collision where kinetic energy is not conserved

concepts.label: Conservation of Energy
concepts.definition: Total energy in an isolated system remains constant

concepts.label: Conservation of Momentum
concepts.definition: Total momentum in an isolated system remains constant

concepts.label: Simple Harmonic Motion
concepts.definition: Oscillatory motion where restoring force is proportional to displacement

concepts.label: Hooke's Law
concepts.definition: Force exerted by spring proportional to displacement from equilibrium

concepts.label: Pendulum
concepts.definition: Weight suspended from a pivot that swings freely under gravity

concepts.label: Gravity
concepts.definition: Fundamental force of attraction between masses

concepts.label: Gravitational Constant
concepts.definition: Fundamental constant G in Newton's law of gravitation

concepts.label: Free Fall
concepts.definition: Motion under influence of gravity alone

concepts.label: Terminal Velocity
concepts.definition: Maximum velocity of an object falling through a fluid

concepts.label: Drag Force
concepts.definition: Resistance force exerted by fluid on moving object

concepts.label: Buoyancy
concepts.definition: Upward force on object submerged in fluid

concepts.label: Archimedes' Principle
concepts.definition: Buoyant force equals weight of displaced fluid

concepts.label: Pascal's Principle
concepts.definition: Pressure applied to confined fluid transmits equally throughout

concepts.label: Bernoulli's Principle
concepts.definition: Increase in fluid speed causes decrease in pressure

concepts.label: Viscosity
concepts.definition: Measure of a fluid's resistance to flow

concepts.label: Laminar Flow
concepts.definition: Smooth fluid flow in parallel layers without disruption

concepts.label: Turbulent Flow
concepts.definition: Chaotic fluid flow with eddies and vortices

concepts.label: Reynolds Number
concepts.definition: Dimensionless number predicting flow regime transition

concepts.label: Surface Tension
concepts.definition: Cohesive force at liquid surface causing it to minimize area

concepts.label: Capillary Action
concepts.definition: Movement of liquid through narrow spaces against gravity

concepts.label: Pressure
concepts.definition: Force per unit area applied perpendicular to a surface

concepts.label: Stress
concepts.definition: Internal forces per unit area within a deformed material

concepts.label: Strain
concepts.definition: Measure of deformation representing displacement between particles

concepts.label: Young's Modulus
concepts.definition: Ratio of stress to strain measuring material stiffness

concepts.label: Elasticity
concepts.definition: Ability of material to return to original shape after deformation

concepts.label: Plasticity
concepts.definition: Tendency of solid material to undergo permanent deformation

concepts.label: Temperature
concepts.definition: Measure of average kinetic energy of particles in a substance

concepts.label: Heat
concepts.definition: Transfer of thermal energy between systems due to temperature difference

concepts.label: First Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: Energy cannot be created or destroyed only transformed

concepts.label: Second Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: Entropy of an isolated system always increases over time

concepts.label: Third Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: Entropy approaches zero as temperature approaches absolute zero

concepts.label: Zeroth Law of Thermodynamics
concepts.definition: If two systems are in thermal equilibrium with a third they are in equilibrium with each other

concepts.label: Entropy
concepts.definition: Measure of disorder or randomness in a thermodynamic system

concepts.label: Enthalpy
concepts.definition: Total heat content of a thermodynamic system at constant pressure

concepts.label: Gibbs Free Energy
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic potential measuring maximum reversible work

concepts.label: Carnot Cycle
concepts.definition: Most efficient thermodynamic cycle operating between two temperatures

concepts.label: Carnot Efficiency
concepts.definition: Maximum theoretical efficiency of a heat engine

concepts.label: Specific Heat Capacity
concepts.definition: Amount of heat needed to raise temperature of unit mass by one degree

concepts.label: Latent Heat
concepts.definition: Energy absorbed or released during a phase change

concepts.label: Conduction
concepts.definition: Heat transfer through direct molecular contact

concepts.label: Convection
concepts.definition: Heat transfer through bulk fluid movement

concepts.label: Radiation
concepts.definition: Heat transfer through electromagnetic waves

concepts.label: Thermal Expansion
concepts.definition: Tendency of matter to increase in volume with temperature

concepts.label: Ideal Gas Law
concepts.definition: PV=nRT relating pressure volume temperature and amount of ideal gas

concepts.label: Boyle's Law
concepts.definition: At constant temperature pressure and volume are inversely proportional

concepts.label: Charles's Law
concepts.definition: At constant pressure volume is proportional to temperature

concepts.label: Avogadro's Law
concepts.definition: Equal volumes of gases at same conditions contain equal numbers of molecules

concepts.label: Boltzmann Constant
concepts.definition: Physical constant relating average kinetic energy to temperature

concepts.label: Stefan-Boltzmann Law
concepts.definition: Total energy radiated is proportional to fourth power of temperature

concepts.label: Black Body Radiation
concepts.definition: Electromagnetic radiation emitted by idealized opaque body

concepts.label: Phase Transition
concepts.definition: Transformation of matter between solid liquid and gas states

concepts.label: Sublimation
concepts.definition: Direct transition from solid to gas phase

concepts.label: Boiling Point
concepts.definition: Temperature at which vapor pressure equals atmospheric pressure

concepts.label: Melting Point
concepts.definition: Temperature at which solid becomes liquid

concepts.label: Triple Point
concepts.definition: Temperature and pressure at which three phases coexist

concepts.label: Critical Point
concepts.definition: End point of phase equilibrium curve beyond which distinct phases do not exist

concepts.label: Heat Engine
concepts.definition: Device converting thermal energy to mechanical work

concepts.label: Refrigeration Cycle
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic cycle that transfers heat from cold to hot reservoir

concepts.label: Adiabatic Process
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic process with no heat transfer

concepts.label: Isothermal Process
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic process at constant temperature

concepts.label: Isobaric Process
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic process at constant pressure

concepts.label: Isochoric Process
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic process at constant volume

concepts.label: Electric Charge
concepts.definition: Fundamental property of matter causing electromagnetic interactions

concepts.label: Coulomb's Law
concepts.definition: Force between charges proportional to product of charges divided by distance squared

concepts.label: Electric Field
concepts.definition: Region of space where electric charge experiences a force

concepts.label: Electric Potential
concepts.definition: Work done per unit charge bringing a test charge from infinity

concepts.label: Voltage
concepts.definition: Difference in electric potential between two points

concepts.label: Electric Current
concepts.definition: Flow of electric charge through a conductor

concepts.label: Ohm's Law
concepts.definition: Current through conductor proportional to voltage across it

concepts.label: Resistance
concepts.definition: Opposition to flow of electric current in a conductor

concepts.label: Capacitance
concepts.definition: Ability of a system to store electric charge

concepts.label: Capacitor
concepts.definition: Device storing electrical energy in an electric field

concepts.label: Inductor
concepts.definition: Component storing energy in a magnetic field when current flows

concepts.label: Inductance
concepts.definition: Property of conductor opposing changes in current

concepts.label: Magnetic Field
concepts.definition: Region of space where moving charges experience a force

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Induction
concepts.definition: Production of voltage by changing magnetic flux

concepts.label: Faraday's Law
concepts.definition: Induced EMF proportional to rate of change of magnetic flux

concepts.label: Lenz's Law
concepts.definition: Direction of induced current opposes change causing it

concepts.label: Ampere's Law
concepts.definition: Magnetic field around current proportional to the current

concepts.label: Maxwell's Equations
concepts.definition: Four fundamental equations describing all electromagnetic phenomena

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Wave
concepts.definition: Self-propagating wave of electric and magnetic fields

concepts.label: Speed of Light
concepts.definition: Constant velocity of electromagnetic waves in vacuum approximately 3e8 m/s

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Spectrum
concepts.definition: Range of all possible electromagnetic radiation frequencies

concepts.label: Radio Waves
concepts.definition: Electromagnetic waves with frequencies from 3 kHz to 300 GHz

concepts.label: Microwaves
concepts.definition: Electromagnetic waves with frequencies from 300 MHz to 300 GHz

concepts.label: Infrared Radiation
concepts.definition: Electromagnetic radiation with wavelength between visible and microwave

concepts.label: Visible Light
concepts.definition: Electromagnetic radiation visible to human eye 380-700 nm

concepts.label: Ultraviolet Radiation
concepts.definition: Electromagnetic radiation with wavelength shorter than visible light

concepts.label: X-rays
concepts.definition: High-energy electromagnetic radiation for medical imaging

concepts.label: Gamma Rays
concepts.definition: Highest-energy electromagnetic radiation from nuclear processes

concepts.label: Refraction
concepts.definition: Bending of light when passing between media of different densities

concepts.label: Reflection
concepts.definition: Bouncing of light off a surface

concepts.label: Diffraction
concepts.definition: Bending of waves around obstacles and through openings

concepts.label: Interference
concepts.definition: Superposition of waves resulting in reinforcement or cancellation

concepts.label: Polarization
concepts.definition: Restriction of electromagnetic wave oscillation to a single plane

concepts.label: Snell's Law
concepts.definition: Ratio of sines of angles of incidence and refraction equals ratio of refractive indices

concepts.label: Total Internal Reflection
concepts.definition: Complete reflection of light at boundary when angle exceeds critical angle

concepts.label: Lens
concepts.definition: Transparent device refracting light to converge or diverge

concepts.label: Mirror
concepts.definition: Surface reflecting light forming images

concepts.label: Semiconductor
concepts.definition: Material with electrical conductivity between conductor and insulator

concepts.label: Diode
concepts.definition: Electronic component allowing current flow in one direction

concepts.label: Transistor
concepts.definition: Semiconductor device used to amplify or switch electronic signals

concepts.label: Superconductivity
concepts.definition: Zero electrical resistance in certain materials below critical temperature

concepts.label: Quantum Mechanics
concepts.definition: Physics of matter and energy at atomic and subatomic scales

concepts.label: Wave-Particle Duality
concepts.definition: Quantum entities exhibit both wave and particle properties

concepts.label: Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle
concepts.definition: Cannot simultaneously know exact position and momentum of a particle

concepts.label: Schrodinger Equation
concepts.definition: Fundamental equation describing quantum state evolution

concepts.label: Quantum Superposition
concepts.definition: Quantum system existing in multiple states simultaneously until measured

concepts.label: Quantum Entanglement
concepts.definition: Correlated quantum states of particles regardless of distance

concepts.label: Quantum Tunneling
concepts.definition: Particle passing through classically forbidden energy barrier

concepts.label: Photoelectric Effect
concepts.definition: Emission of electrons from surface when light strikes it

concepts.label: Planck's Constant
concepts.definition: Fundamental constant relating photon energy to frequency

concepts.label: Photon
concepts.definition: Quantum of electromagnetic radiation with zero rest mass

concepts.label: Electron
concepts.definition: Subatomic particle with negative charge orbiting atomic nucleus

concepts.label: Proton
concepts.definition: Subatomic particle with positive charge in atomic nucleus

concepts.label: Neutron
concepts.definition: Electrically neutral subatomic particle in atomic nucleus

concepts.label: Quark
concepts.definition: Elementary particle and fundamental constituent of matter

concepts.label: Lepton
concepts.definition: Elementary particle not subject to strong nuclear force

concepts.label: Boson
concepts.definition: Particle with integer spin mediating fundamental forces

concepts.label: Fermion
concepts.definition: Particle with half-integer spin making up matter

concepts.label: Higgs Boson
concepts.definition: Particle associated with Higgs field giving mass to other particles

concepts.label: Higgs Field
concepts.definition: Quantum field permeating space giving mass to fundamental particles

concepts.label: Standard Model
concepts.definition: Theory describing fundamental particles and three of four forces

concepts.label: Strong Nuclear Force
concepts.definition: Force binding quarks together within protons and neutrons

concepts.label: Weak Nuclear Force
concepts.definition: Force responsible for radioactive beta decay

concepts.label: Electromagnetic Force
concepts.definition: Force between electrically charged particles

concepts.label: Pauli Exclusion Principle
concepts.definition: No two fermions can occupy the same quantum state

concepts.label: Spin
concepts.definition: Intrinsic angular momentum of subatomic particles

concepts.label: Quantum Number
concepts.definition: Value describing quantized property of a quantum system

concepts.label: Orbital
concepts.definition: Region of space where electron is likely to be found

concepts.label: Wave Function
concepts.definition: Mathematical description of quantum state of a system

concepts.label: Quantum Decoherence
concepts.definition: Loss of quantum coherence through interaction with environment

concepts.label: Quantum Computing
concepts.definition: Computation using quantum mechanical phenomena like superposition

concepts.label: Qubit
concepts.definition: Basic unit of quantum information analogous to classical bit

concepts.label: Bell's Theorem
concepts.definition: No local hidden variable theory can reproduce all quantum predictions

concepts.label: Copenhagen Interpretation
concepts.definition: Quantum mechanics interpretation where measurement collapses wave function

concepts.label: Many-Worlds Interpretation
concepts.definition: Every quantum measurement causes universe branching

concepts.label: Quantum Field Theory
concepts.definition: Framework combining quantum mechanics and special relativity

concepts.label: Antimatter
concepts.definition: Matter composed of antiparticles with opposite charges

concepts.label: Positron
concepts.definition: Antiparticle of electron with positive charge

concepts.label: Neutrino
concepts.definition: Electrically neutral subatomic particle with very small mass

concepts.label: Gluon
concepts.definition: Boson mediating the strong nuclear force

concepts.label: W Boson
concepts.definition: Carrier particle of weak nuclear force

concepts.label: Z Boson
concepts.definition: Neutral carrier particle of weak nuclear force

concepts.label: Special Relativity
concepts.definition: Einstein's theory of space and time for uniform motion

concepts.label: General Relativity
concepts.definition: Einstein's theory of gravity as spacetime curvature

concepts.label: Time Dilation
concepts.definition: Slowing of time for objects moving at high velocities

concepts.label: Length Contraction
concepts.definition: Shortening of objects moving at relativistic speeds

concepts.label: Mass-Energy Equivalence
concepts.definition: E=mc² relating mass and energy

concepts.label: Spacetime
concepts.definition: Four-dimensional continuum of space and time

concepts.label: Gravitational Lensing
concepts.definition: Bending of light by massive objects warping spacetime

concepts.label: Black Hole
concepts.definition: Region of spacetime where gravity prevents anything from escaping

concepts.label: Event Horizon
concepts.definition: Boundary of black hole beyond which nothing can escape

concepts.label: Singularity
concepts.definition: Point of infinite density at center of black hole

concepts.label: Gravitational Waves
concepts.definition: Ripples in spacetime caused by accelerating massive objects

concepts.label: Frame of Reference
concepts.definition: Coordinate system for measuring motion and position

concepts.label: Lorentz Transformation
concepts.definition: Mathematical transformation between inertial reference frames

concepts.label: Twin Paradox
concepts.definition: Thought experiment illustrating time dilation in special relativity

concepts.label: Cosmological Constant
concepts.definition: Energy density of empty space in Einstein's field equations

concepts.label: Dark Matter
concepts.definition: Hypothetical matter accounting for gravitational effects not explained by visible matter

concepts.label: Dark Energy
concepts.definition: Hypothetical energy driving accelerating expansion of universe

concepts.label: Big Bang
concepts.definition: Theory that universe expanded from extremely hot dense state

concepts.label: Cosmic Microwave Background
concepts.definition: Remnant radiation from early universe detectable in all directions

concepts.label: Hubble's Law
concepts.definition: Galaxies recede at velocity proportional to their distance

concepts.label: Redshift
concepts.definition: Increase in wavelength of light from receding objects

concepts.label: Blueshift
concepts.definition: Decrease in wavelength of light from approaching objects

concepts.label: Doppler Effect
concepts.definition: Change in frequency of wave due to relative motion of source and observer

concepts.label: Sound Wave
concepts.definition: Mechanical wave propagating through a medium as pressure variations

concepts.label: Frequency
concepts.definition: Number of wave cycles per unit time measured in Hertz

concepts.label: Wavelength
concepts.definition: Distance between successive peaks of a wave

concepts.label: Amplitude
concepts.definition: Maximum displacement from equilibrium in a wave

concepts.label: Resonance
concepts.definition: Increase in amplitude when frequency matches natural frequency

concepts.label: Standing Wave
concepts.definition: Wave pattern with fixed nodes and antinodes

concepts.label: Harmonics
concepts.definition: Integer multiples of a fundamental frequency

concepts.label: Decibel
concepts.definition: Logarithmic unit measuring sound intensity

concepts.label: Ultrasound
concepts.definition: Sound waves with frequency above human hearing range

concepts.label: Infrasound
concepts.definition: Sound waves with frequency below human hearing range

concepts.label: Nuclear Fission
concepts.definition: Splitting of heavy atomic nucleus into lighter nuclei releasing energy

concepts.label: Nuclear Fusion
concepts.definition: Combining light atomic nuclei into heavier nucleus releasing energy

concepts.label: Radioactivity
concepts.definition: Spontaneous emission of radiation from unstable atomic nuclei

concepts.label: Alpha Decay
concepts.definition: Radioactive decay emitting helium-4 nucleus

concepts.label: Beta Decay
concepts.definition: Radioactive decay emitting electron or positron

concepts.label: Half-Life
concepts.definition: Time for half of radioactive atoms to decay

concepts.label: Nuclear Reactor
concepts.definition: Device sustaining controlled nuclear chain reaction

concepts.label: Isotope
concepts.definition: Atoms of same element with different numbers of neutrons

concepts.label: Radioactive Dating
concepts.definition: Method of determining age using radioactive decay rates

concepts.label: Chain Reaction
concepts.definition: Self-sustaining sequence of nuclear fission reactions

concepts.label: Laser
concepts.definition: Device emitting coherent monochromatic light through stimulated emission

concepts.label: Fiber Optics
concepts.definition: Technology transmitting light through thin glass or plastic fibers

concepts.label: Holography
concepts.definition: Technique recording and reconstructing three-dimensional light fields

concepts.label: Plasma
concepts.definition: Fourth state of matter consisting of ionized gas

concepts.label: Bose-Einstein Condensate
concepts.definition: State of matter at near absolute zero where atoms behave as single entity

concepts.label: Piezoelectricity
concepts.definition: Electric charge accumulating in response to mechanical stress

concepts.label: Thermoelectricity
concepts.definition: Direct conversion of temperature difference to electric voltage

concepts.label: Atom
concepts.definition: Smallest unit of a chemical element retaining its properties

concepts.label: Molecule
concepts.definition: Group of atoms bonded together forming smallest unit of a compound

concepts.label: Element
concepts.definition: Pure substance made of one type of atom

concepts.label: Compound
concepts.definition: Substance made of two or more elements chemically bonded

concepts.label: Chemical Bond
concepts.definition: Attractive force holding atoms together in molecules

concepts.label: Covalent Bond
concepts.definition: Chemical bond formed by sharing electron pairs between atoms

concepts.label: Ionic Bond
concepts.definition: Chemical bond formed by transfer of electrons between atoms

concepts.label: Metallic Bond
concepts.definition: Bond in metals where electrons are shared in a sea of delocalized electrons

concepts.label: Hydrogen Bond
concepts.definition: Weak bond between hydrogen and electronegative atom like oxygen or nitrogen

concepts.label: Van der Waals Forces
concepts.definition: Weak intermolecular forces from temporary dipoles

concepts.label: Electronegativity
concepts.definition: Tendency of atom to attract shared electrons in a bond

concepts.label: Ion
concepts.definition: Atom or molecule with net electric charge from gaining or losing electrons

concepts.label: Cation
concepts.definition: Positively charged ion formed by losing electrons

concepts.label: Anion
concepts.definition: Negatively charged ion formed by gaining electrons

concepts.label: Periodic Table
concepts.definition: Tabular arrangement of elements by atomic number and properties

concepts.label: Atomic Number
concepts.definition: Number of protons in the nucleus of an atom

concepts.label: Atomic Mass
concepts.definition: Total mass of protons and neutrons in an atom

concepts.label: Valence Electron
concepts.definition: Electron in outermost shell participating in chemical bonding

concepts.label: Electron Shell
concepts.definition: Region around nucleus where electrons are found

concepts.label: Oxidation
concepts.definition: Loss of electrons or increase in oxidation state

concepts.label: Reduction
concepts.definition: Gain of electrons or decrease in oxidation state

concepts.label: Redox Reaction
concepts.definition: Chemical reaction involving transfer of electrons

concepts.label: Acid
concepts.definition: Substance that donates protons or accepts electron pairs

concepts.label: Base
concepts.definition: Substance that accepts protons or donates electron pairs

concepts.label: pH Scale
concepts.definition: Measure of hydrogen ion concentration indicating acidity or basicity

concepts.label: Buffer Solution
concepts.definition: Solution resisting changes in pH when acid or base is added

concepts.label: Catalyst
concepts.definition: Substance increasing reaction rate without being consumed

concepts.label: Enzyme
concepts.definition: Biological catalyst usually a protein accelerating biochemical reactions

concepts.label: Chemical Equilibrium
concepts.definition: State where forward and reverse reaction rates are equal

concepts.label: Le Chatelier's Principle
concepts.definition: System at equilibrium shifts to counteract applied changes

concepts.label: Exothermic Reaction
concepts.definition: Chemical reaction releasing heat to surroundings

concepts.label: Endothermic Reaction
concepts.definition: Chemical reaction absorbing heat from surroundings

concepts.label: Activation Energy
concepts.definition: Minimum energy required for a chemical reaction to occur

concepts.label: Reaction Rate
concepts.definition: Speed at which reactants are converted to products

concepts.label: Molarity
concepts.definition: Concentration measured as moles of solute per liter of solution

concepts.label: Mole
concepts.definition: Amount of substance containing Avogadro's number of particles

concepts.label: Avogadro's Number
concepts.definition: 6.022 × 10^23 particles per mole

concepts.label: Stoichiometry
concepts.definition: Calculation of quantities in chemical reactions

concepts.label: Solution
concepts.definition: Homogeneous mixture of two or more substances

concepts.label: Solvent
concepts.definition: Substance in which solute dissolves usually the larger component

concepts.label: Solute
concepts.definition: Substance dissolved in a solvent

concepts.label: Solubility
concepts.definition: Maximum amount of solute that can dissolve in a solvent

concepts.label: Precipitation
concepts.definition: Formation of insoluble solid from mixing solutions

concepts.label: Electrolysis
concepts.definition: Using electric current to drive non-spontaneous chemical reaction

concepts.label: Electrolyte
concepts.definition: Substance producing ions when dissolved enabling electrical conduction

concepts.label: Combustion
concepts.definition: Rapid chemical reaction with oxygen producing heat and light

concepts.label: Photosynthesis
concepts.definition: Process by which plants convert light energy to chemical energy

concepts.label: Cellular Respiration
concepts.definition: Metabolic process converting glucose and oxygen to energy

concepts.label: Fermentation
concepts.definition: Anaerobic metabolic process producing alcohol or lactic acid
